task_completion.description: Load the image sequence into Blender's Movie Clip Editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Movie Clip Editor is open and displays the synthetic footage, which visually consists of black markers on a white ground plane.

task_completion.description: Track at least 15 features throughout the shot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Movie Clip Editor shows numerous tracking markers (at least 15) placed on the black dots in the footage, with visible tracking paths/trails indicating they have been tracked across multiple frames.

task_completion.description: Solve the 3D camera trajectory with a solve error of less than 1.0 pixel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Solve Error' value displayed in the Movie Clip Editor (typically in the top right corner of the footage area or the tool shelf) shows a numerical value less than 1.00 px.

task_completion.description: Apply the tracking solution to the scene camera.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Viewport shows the scene camera with a 'Camera Solver' constraint applied in the properties panel, or evidence of 'Setup Tracking Scene' being used (e.g., camera background images enabled, 3D markers visible in the viewport).

task_completion.description: Save the solved project to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar displays the filename 'tracking_solved.blend' or the Blender file browser is open showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender graphical user interface to perform the tracking and solving.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with Blender's Movie Clip Editor, 3D Viewport, and Properties panels, rather than just executing a pre-written Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The tracking markers and solve error are genuinely generated by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Tracking paths follow the actual movement of the black markers in the footage, and the solve error is displayed in the standard Blender UI font and location, not typed into a text editor or overlaid.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved the Blender project file through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save operation is visible via Blender's 'Save As' dialog or the top menu bar, rather than just using the 'touch' command in the terminal to create an empty file named 'tracking_solved.blend'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The footage is located at '/home/ga/footage/' and consists of 40 frames (1-40) of synthetic tracking footage featuring black markers on a white ground plane. The agent must track at least 15 features and achieve a solve error of less than 1.0 pixel. The final output must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/tracking_solved.blend'.
